records.ety: [Cf. F. mcanisme, L. mechanisma. See Mechanic.]

defs: The arrangement or relation of the parts of a machine; the parts of a machine, taken collectively; the arrangement or relation of the parts of anything as adapted to produce an effect; as, the mechanism of a watch; the mechanism of a sewing machine; the mechanism of a seed pod.
The series of causal relations that operate to produce an effect in any system; as, the mechanism of a chemical reaction.

quotes: He acknowledges nothing besides matter and motion; so that all must be performed either by mechanism or accident.

defs: An ideal machine; a combination of movable bodies constituting a machine, but considered only with regard to relative movements.
